programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The tragic Greek myth of Actaeon, who stumbled upon the goddess Artemis; the Celtic Otherworld, an idyllic place of magic and beauty; the Kraken; venturing into the woods of the Brothers Grimm.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Romulus and Remus; Vikings and the Valkyries who ushered them to the next world; the Trojan War; how Homer's "The Iliad" influenced Alexander the Great; Vlad the Impaler.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The medieval romance of Tristan and Isolde; how the abandonment of Dido by Aeneas paved the way for Rome; Norse attitudes of sex and marriage in "Lay of Thrym"; Medea murders her children.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The role of myths and legends in the establishment of the rules of society, as well as the punishments that result from the violation of the rules.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The ancient Greek myth of Antigone, who defied laws to bury her dead brother; the Norse god Baldr; flood narratives found in myth; fascination with the undead; history of the vampire.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The strange Slavic story of Ivan and Koschei the Deathless; the origins of King Arthur and the wizard Merlin; Odysseus resists the song of the Sirens; Sigurd and his battle with the dragon Fafnir.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The tragic Greek myth of Actaeon, who stumbled upon the goddess Artemis; the Celtic Otherworld, an idyllic place of magic and beauty; the Kraken; venturing into the woods of the Brothers Grimm.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Romulus and Remus; Vikings and the Valkyries who ushered them to the next world; the Trojan War; how Homer's "The Iliad" influenced Alexander the Great; Vlad the Impaler.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The medieval romance of Tristan and Isolde; how the abandonment of Dido by Aeneas paved the way for Rome; Norse attitudes of sex and marriage in "Lay of Thrym"; Medea murders her children.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The role of myths and legends in the establishment of the rules of society, as well as the punishments that result from the violation of the rules.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: The ancient Greek myth of Antigone, who defied laws to bury her dead brother; the Norse god Baldr; flood narratives found in myth; fascination with the undead; history of the vampire.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: At the time of Jack the Ripper, London was also terrorized by the elusive `Borough Poisoner.'

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: In 1910, the infamous Dr. Crippen killed his wife and buried her in the basement.

programme.title: Besieged Fortresses
programme.desc: When Saladin invades Jerusalem, Philip Augustus and Richard the Lionheart put their rivalry aside to fight for Saint-John of Acre, a highly strategic port in the Holy land.

programme.title: Besieged Fortresses
programme.desc: Despite an overwhelming force of men, the Ottomans, led by Soliman are unable to shake the fortifications of Rhodes.

programme.title: Besieged Fortresses
programme.desc: Suleiman the Magnificent, Sultan of the Ottoman Empire, wants control of the Mediterranean Sea.

programme.title: Besieged Fortresses
programme.desc: Sultan Mehmed leads an army, and a stunning new superweapon, against a last remnant of Christianity.

programme.title: Vikings: The Lost Kingdom
programme.desc: 2018, Gjellestad, Norway. Archaeologists made the discovery of a lifetime: a 20-meter long, 1200-year-old Viking funeral ship.

programme.title: The True Story of Pirates
programme.desc: A group of international archaeologists and historians paint a new portrait of piracy in the Indian Ocean.

programme.title: Besieged Fortresses
programme.desc: When Saladin invades Jerusalem, Philip Augustus and Richard the Lionheart put their rivalry aside to fight for Saint-John of Acre, a highly strategic port in the Holy land.

programme.desc: Sultan Mehmed leads an army, and a stunning new superweapon, against a last remnant of Christianity.

programme.desc: A group of international archaeologists and historians paint a new portrait of piracy in the Indian Ocean.

programme.title: Besieged Fortresses
programme.desc: When Saladin invades Jerusalem, Philip Augustus and Richard the Lionheart put their rivalry aside to fight for Saint-John of Acre, a highly strategic port in the Holy land.

programme.title: NYC Revealed
programme.desc: Getting water from upstate reservoirs to the taps of over 8.5 million people in New York City is no easy feat.
